title: daemon/relay.rs dispatch_peer_receipt allows 'sent' but PeerReceipt wire type only carries 'delivered'|'read'
description: Trigger: dispatch_peer_receipt() in daemon/src/relay.rs line 661 validates receipt_type against the set {'sent', 'delivered', 'read'}. But the ClearMessage::PeerReceipt deserializer in core/src/messages.rs (deserialize_receipt_type) only accepts {'delivered', 'read'} - 'sent' is rejected at the wire boundary before dispatch_peer_receipt is ever called. The 'sent' case in dispatch_peer_receipt is therefore dead code. Bad behavior: no current bug, but if store.update_message_delivery_state() is called with 'sent', the delivery_state column receives a value that the wire serializer can never produce, creating an invisible state inconsistency. Maintenance risk: a future refactor that calls dispatch_peer_receipt directly (bypassing deserialization) or loosens the deserializer could write 'sent' into the DB. Fix: remove 'sent' from the valid set in dispatch_peer_receipt so both layers use the same allowed values.
